synthesis.summary: ARM surged 15% today with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The elevated relative volume (1.13x) and RSI approaching overbought territory (68.3) suggest institutional or options-driven buying, but without a visible catalyst — earnings, guidance, or analyst action — the driver is unidentifiable from available data.

ALAB surged 17.71% today with nearly double its average volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The 28% gain over five days and overbought RSI of 76 suggest sustained momentum-driven buying, but the catalyst — whether earnings, a contract win, analyst upgrade, or sector rotation — is not visible in the provided news feed.

ENPH is up 13.7% today and 26.6% over the past five days with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The relative volume is nearly in line with normal levels (1.05x), which makes a clear catalyst-driven explanation unlikely — this looks more consistent with short covering, sector rotation into solar/clean energy names, or options-driven momentum than any identifiable fundamental development.

Given the stock is down ~13% over the past 5 days, this looks like a short-term bounce or short-covering rally rather than a catalyst-driven move — but that is speculative without data to confirm it.
